title: Multi-token Markov Game with Switching Costs

title: Simple Fixes that Accommodate Switching Costs in Multi-Armed Bandit

abstract: <jats:p xml:lang="fr"><abstract><p>The multi-leader-follower group consensus issue of a class of stochastic time-delay multi-agent systems subject to Markov switching topology is investigated. The purpose is to determine a distributed control protocol to make sure that the followers' states converge in mean square to a convex hull generated by the leaders' states. Through a model transformation, the problem is transformed into a mean-square stability issue of a new system. Then, an easy-to-check sufficient condition for the solvability of the multi-leader-follower group consensus issue is proposed by utilizing the Lyapunov stability theory, graph theory, as well as several inequality techniques. It is shown that the required feedback gain can be acquired once the condition is satisfied. Finally, an example is used to illustrate the effectiveness of the control protocol.</p></abstract></jats:p>
title: Multi-leader-follower group consensus of stochastic time-delay multi-agent systems subject to Markov switching topology

publisher: Akdeniz Spor Bilimleri Dergisi (Mediterranean Journal of Sport Science)
abstract: <jats:p xml:lang="en">In this study, the effects of the three-point rule in football on the average goals per game and the predictability of the matches were investigated by using the Markov-Regime Switching Model. The results obtained in the analysis covering 61 seasons and 3 teams in the example of the Turkish First Division Football League showed that the three-point rule increased the average goals per game, as expected. However, the effects of the three-point rule on the predictability of matches are different for each team.</jats:p>
title: Three-Point Rule, Average Goals Per Game and Predictability in Football: A Long-Term Analysis with The Markov-Regime Switching Approach
Futbolda Üç Puan Kuralı, Gol Sayısı ve Öngörülebilirlik: Markov-Rejim Değişim Modeli ile Uzun Dönemli Bir Analiz

title: Artificial Intelligence Meets Computational Intelligence: Multi-Commodity Price Volatility Accuracy Forecast with Variants of Markov-Switching-GARCH-Type-Extreme Learning Machines Hybridization Framework
